rating.subject: Sparrow Payments API
rating.rationale: Held at unacceptable by a single binding constraint: a live payment-gateway credential is committed to the repository. Rotating and removing it would lift the root off the floor, but the Webhooks delivery deduplication gap would then bind at minimum.

notAssessed: Reconciliation runs daily and flags drift

findingSummaries.summary: A live gateway secret key is committed in plaintext; value withheld.
A reconcile entrypoint exists, but no scheduled run output, log, or report was available.
Deduplication state is retained for the same 24-hour window as retries, so late re-enqueue can duplicate delivery.

recommendations.doneCriterion: The committed-credentials requirement reaches target: no live credential is present in the working tree and the previously exposed key has been revoked.
The reconciliation requirement becomes assessable and reaches at least the acceptable floor.
The webhook-delivery deduplication requirement reaches target within the declared retry window.

limitations: Standard effort did not seek broader failure-injection and step-up-control evidence required for outstanding ratings.
The committed-credential finding reflects the tracked working tree at the evaluated commit.
Transfer idempotency and webhook deduplication each rest on one replay/redelivery test, not sustained concurrency or fault-injection soak.
